Bad. Very bad game. Though the graphics and the first person concept are pretty good, the game flaws in too many things. The compass only covers 180º, something very annoying in combat because you cannot order high variations of the course. You cannot order to cease fire to you deck gun, so they will waste all the ammo, etc... And what's that thing of the ships health bar? Is this an arcade? If you have a tanker in flames all across the ship, do not think it will sink or explode, you must hammer that ship until you finish its health. Then i guess the fire is just ornamental... Those are just SOME of the high flaws this game has. 
If you like a submarine simulator, buy Silent Hunter Wolves of Pacific, it is a way far better game...

And now it doesn't ever start, you would need to go through a lot of cracking and file changing and editing just to make it work to get around the fact that you need Ubisoft to play the game, and Ubisoft doesn't support the game anymore.

This title is quite the older sub sim but it is still the best sim even to date. Despite several newer titles, SH5 is by far the most stable and for the most part very good graphics. Price is nothing to date but worth every penny. I guess companies just don't know how to make a good sub sim. Even put up against the newest such as Uboat SH5 still holds its ground.

Review Title: Broken Periscopes After Last Update – Stay Away
Review Body:
“Silent Hunter 5 once delivered immersive WWII U‑boat gameplay—but Ubisoft’s latest update broke the game entirely. Launching via Steam freezes at Connect, and even logging in no longer helps. What worked—Ubisoft Connect relinking or community patches—fails now entirely. Without access to mod tools or earlier versions, this feels abandoned. Shame; beneath it all was a deep, immersive sim star. As of July 2025, do not buy—you can’t even play.”

This game sadly never lived up to expectations. However, if you are looking for a good submarine simulator, buy UBOAT. It's what Silent Hunter V should have been, and is an excellent spiritual successor to the Silent Hunter series. Highly recommend it.

Cannot reccomend this game why?

This awesome game is tied to Ubisoft uplay/ubisoft connect. To get the game to run you will need to 

1. download Ubisoft connect 

2. uninstall uplay 

3. Sign into or make a Ubisoft account

4. Take your steam game key and redeem it on Ubisoft launcher 

5. You can now play this game on steam

Seems easy enough right? Well Ubisoft is tanking faster right now than a merchant ship hit with a spread of 4 torpedos. Some think they will either go bankrupt or be bought out by another company in the coming years some say as early as 2025 but it’s just speculation.

 If that happens for however long you will presumably not be able to play this game due to it needing to connect to Ubisoft servers to launch the game. 

You can kind of see this Happening with other Ubisoft titles from around this time with similar DRM measures. Like Assasins creed 2 where you cannot play paid dlc anymore due to servers for that being shut down around 2022. Or games like The crew no longer being playable at all. Or farcry 3 and assassins creed 3 where dlc cant be played anymore

If the servers go down you simply you won’t be able to play your game anymore  

(This is more or less a buyer beware your game could potentially become unplayable in the near future. Or atleast unplayable for an unknown period of time if Ubisoft goes bankrupt/closes down/sells its company off)

A game that requires hacks and tweaks to even run is not a game. After installing it said it cannot connect to the Ubisoft server, but googling found that I needed to install a new version. Game is dependant on people's ability to hunt the internet for a solution. First red flag.

Second red flag is the intro video plays silently in a corner of the screen, then crashes. Again, a google reveals this is a known issue since 2019 and a mod fixes it. 

So not only has is been known that there is an issue, but there is a mod to fix it. A mod. Not an official game patch, but some guy has decided to make his or her own fix. 

I recommend you go and play a game that is still valid and not hidden in shame like a ginger step-child.

Silent Hunter 5®: Battle of the Atlantic - Dive Into Submarine Warfare 🌊🚢🎯

Ahoy, submarine enthusiasts! Silent Hunter 5®: Battle of the Atlantic is your ticket to commanding a German U-boat in the most thrilling underwater warfare experience ever crafted. Step behind the periscope and immerse yourself in the depths of the Atlantic Ocean and the Mediterranean Sea, where you'll face off against the formidable Allied Forces in some of the most iconic naval battles of World War II. 🌊🔭

The best-selling submarine series continues its legacy with a bang, offering an incredibly detailed and realistic simulation of submarine warfare. Whether you're meticulously plotting your course, managing your crew, or engaging in intense naval skirmishes, every moment in Silent Hunter 5 is a deep dive into strategic depth and immersive action. 🎯🕵️‍♂️

From the high tension of evading enemy depth charges to the satisfaction of a successful torpedo strike, this game captures the essence of submarine combat with stunning accuracy. The atmosphere is palpable, and the historical context adds layers of authenticity that make every mission feel like a true adventure under the waves. 🚢💥

In summary, Silent Hunter 5®: Battle of the Atlantic is a must-play for fans of submarine simulations and naval history alike. It’s a grand voyage into the silent depths of the ocean where your strategic prowess and command skills will be put to the ultimate test. Dive in and take command! 🌊⚓🎮

I like the game overall, but I'm not 100% a fan of some of the features that you have to access skeuomorphically, like actually, manually descending from the conning tower and walking over to the hydrophone, instead of just using the intercom to ask the operator what's the status.  For a game that's more than ten years old, the graphics look darned good.

I do wish this was not the last game in the silent hunter series. I think silent hunters 4 will still go down as the superior game to the series and that says a lot seeing how that game is also pretty buggy at times and silent hunters 3 though good is so out of date it cant really be enjoyed on newer pcs.

That said I do think this game's modding community is where this entry shines the most. Silent hunters 4 has a similar thing of the modding community keeping that old game alive and this game is no different with silent hunters 5 having wolves of steel basically making the game how it should of been on released rather than what came out

Do I suggest you buy and play the base game... not really its just not a worth while entry into the sub sim gaming and the more recent release of Uboat has kinda filled its role and better but with wolves of steel I feel this game suddenly becomes that thing that I sometimes wish Uboat was.

While the entire series is pretty iconic, of all of the Silent Hunter games, I feel like this one is overall "the best" in the present day. Though SHI3 is probably the most realistic of the bunch, the graphics are very dated, and it's tough to play on high-resolution screens. SH4 improved on that, but then made the targeting (even manual) far too "arcadey", and the control scheme is still difficult to use without making tons of silly mistakes. And with 5, everything just feels easier to use. The controls don't make me do things I didn't expect to happen, the targeting (while still arcadey) is more intuitive, the UI more compact, and the game doesn't look too bad at all either. The music and sounds are really enjoyable to listen to as well. I'd say, pick this one up (maybe on sale) if you get the chance, and you're a pretty strong naval combat fan.
